# Transcrição: 5. The Winner's Exchange

- **Fonte:** 5. The Winner's Exchange
- **Duração:** 04:18
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hey, this is called the Winner's Exchange. You know, once someone is actually closed and agrees to own your product or service, at that moment that person is now carrying something with them that they previously did not have. If what they received is actually worth more than what was given, you, your product, your service, idea, whatever, if that is worth more than what they gave, then you will be able to repeat this process over and over and over again. This is what is called the Winner's Exchange. When there's a Winner's Exchange, the person you closed will not only feel like the exchange was a win for them, but they would also, not just a win for them, but they would also want to go do this, repeat this activity again, because the perceived value received was greater than the value given. For instance, I'll give you an example of a company that is operating in the Winner's Exchange on a daily basis. Okay? This is the online shoe company. Great examples of Winner's Exchange. They promised me free shipping both ways. Price has nothing to do with the exchange with these people. The win with Zappos is removing any risk I have of loss from the decision-making process, and I don't have to leave my home. Obviously, someone at Zappos surveyed enough people to determine that price was not the problem with purchasing shoes. The problem with purchasing shoes anywhere, particularly online, was rather the fear of making a mistake and then stuck with the cost of shipping. All right? I'll give you an example, another example. I have a book here. It's $30, okay? It's a $30 book. You give me $30 for the book. I give you the book. What is the exchange? What is the book worth to you if you gave me $30? Most people say, well, it was worth $30. The truth is, you wouldn't close if it was worth $30. You would only give me $30 if you thought the book was worth more than $30, and I would only give you the book if I thought your $30 was worth more than my book. Do you understand? You have to get to the Winner's Exchange. See, look, watch what happens, okay? I get your $30, and you don't read the book. The truth is, I didn't really close you. Listen to this. You get the book, I get the $30. Oh, I closed the deal. You didn't read the book. I didn't close you because I never really got the Winner's Exchange. You will therefore, I sold you, got the money, but I didn't close you on using the data, learning the data, taking the time to really understand it and read it. You picked it up, you read it, you put it down. That means I didn't really close you. I got your money. This is a problem with most individuals in businesses today. They sell ideas. They pump people. They convince them to do something, but don't follow up to make sure the exchange, the Winner's Exchange, gets in. See, if I don't have a Winner's Exchange, you're not going to tell me. You're not going to tell anybody about my book because the book won't help you if you don't read it. It is my responsibility as the seller to make sure you don't just pay for the book, but

## [03:01 - 04:18]

that I get you in a Winner's Exchange. See, when I get more than I give, I want to tell other people about it. I sell you on the book. I get you to read it. It changes your production level. You go from $30 to the book being worth $30,000 to you. You'll tell everybody. Winner's Exchange. It's me and you. Benefits everyone you sell. The only way you can become truly successful is not just to sell and close a deal, but to make sure you have the Winner's Exchange in. Look, I want to feel good. You want to feel good. I feel good about them. They feel good about me. We've got a Winner's Exchange. I leave and I want you, if you bought from me, to promote me. They leave. I want them to promote me. Your customer closes, buys. You want them to promote you. This is called the Winner's Exchange. You have to get it in. At the end of this segment, I want you to actually look at whatever product you sell. How can you get to make sure someone just loves what you did? You got 50 grand from them. You have to deliver more than 50. Figure out what that is. It's not money. Figure out what things you can do that add value to your proposition and put you in a Winner's Exchange.
